chunks.text: Sky Computing Lab receives NVIDIA DGX B200 for AI research. This week, the Sky Computing Lab at UC Berkeley EECS became the first research institution in the nation to receive NVIDIA’s cutting-edge DGX B200 system. The DGX B200 is a powerful tool for advancing AI research, designed to handle a wide range of AI workloads.

NBC News: Ion Stoica on DeepSeek. EECS Professor Ion Stoica discusses DeepSeek with Gadi Schwartz on NBC News NOW. Ion Stoica featured on Sequoia Capital’s Training Data podcast. EECS Professor and Databricks co-founder Ion Stoica is featured on Sequoia Capital’s Training Data podcast.

UC Berkeley announces AI Center of Excellence. Graduate student team leads for the vLLM project: Woosuk Kwon, Zhuohan Li, Simon Mo. The center will serve as a hub for cutting-edge applied research in generative AI (GenAI), drawing on the collective knowledge and resources of Intel and UC Berkeley.

Physical Intelligence, a Robot A.I. Specialist, Raises Millions From Bezos. Physical Intelligence, an artificial intelligence startup co-founded by EECS Associate Professor Sergey Levine, announced it had raised $400 million in financing from major investors. The company aims to develop foundational software designed to be compatible with any robot.

Paul Gray wins NAE Simon Ramo Founders Award. EECS Professor Emeritus Paul Gray has won the National Academy of Engineering (NAE) Simon Ramo Founders Award. The Simon Ramo Founders Award, established in 1965, is one of the NAE’s highest honors.

Opaque Launches New Platform For Running AI Workloads on Encrypted Data. The Confidential AI platform was developed at the Berkeley RISELab, a world-renowned lab known for developing technologies such as Apache Spark and Databricks. It was at this lab where the breakthrough MC2 platform was created, incubated, and open-sourced.

Governor Newsom convenes GenAI leaders for landmark summit. California Governor Gavin Newsom and leaders representing technology, government, academia, labor, civic organizations convened at the Joint California Summit on Generative AI. The summit included CDSS Dean and EECS Professor Jennifer Chayes, EECS Associate Professor Matei Zaharia, and EECS alumna.

The 2024 EECS Distinguished Alumni. The EECS Department is proud to present the 2024 Distinguished Alumni Awards in Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences. The Department has established a Distinguished Alumni Award to recognize valuable contributions made by the distinguished alumni of Berkeley.

How to keep AI from killing us all. As companies build ever more powerful AI systems — with capabilities that rival or even exceed those of humans — research into AI safety is lagging far behind. “I think the only way forward is to figure out how to make AI safety a condition of doing business,” said UC.
